05-REQ-027a.note: forfeitedTeamIds re-authored as the game record's unseated-teams marking (game-lifecycle/game-record stores it; this capability owns its scoring meaning). Forfeit = loss with score 0, distinguishable from a played loss by the marking, not the value; reflected in scoring, ranking/leaderboard, and replay surfaces. Also covers the forfeit-scoring aspect of 03-REQ-056 (that id mapped by migrate-game-lifecycle, which owns the refusal transitions).

05-REQ-059.note: Tournament = configured number of rounds, each a distinct game with its own record (game-lifecycle/game-record) and its own freshly provisioned instance (game-lifecycle/instance-per-game). Participant set fixed at tournament start (rooms-and-matchmaking/team-enrolment); a forfeit never unseats a team for later rounds (#forfeit-does-not-unseat).

05-REQ-060.note: Round N+1 begins after round N finishes plus the configured interlude; Convex is the sole authority for scheduling and initiating every round — no human initiates a scheduled round. Scheduled rounds consult no readiness gate (#no-ready-check-between-rounds, the constraint-mined lead).

05-REQ-061.note: First round begins no earlier than the tournament's scheduled start time, regardless of team readiness. The tournament is initiated through the ordinary room gate (rooms-and-matchmaking/game-start-gate); the scheduled start defers the launch, never the other way around.

05-REQ-062.note: Rounds inherit the gameplay configuration captured from the tournament's opening game at tournament creation; the inherited set excludes the tournament meta-parameters (round count, interlude, scheduled start time), which are properties of the tournament as a whole.

05-REQ-063.note: No auto-created game after the final round — the concrete 'a format may govern that none follows' arm of game-lifecycle/successor-auto-creation (dedupe seam with 05-REQ-039, mapped by migrate-game-lifecycle).

05-REQ-064.note: Whole-event roster freeze: first round entering play until final round finished, interludes included; phrased as this capability's own enclosing-competitive-engagement extension composing with team-management/roster-freeze's 'holdable longer' clause without a cross-DAG citation (dedupe: the 05-REQ-013 tournament sentence). Anchored to the tournament's in-progress state, never derived from a round's playing status. Subject to this change's Open Question 1 (authored per 05-REVIEW-003 option B; confirm vs the 03-REVIEW-006 unresolved sub-question).

03-REVIEW-012.note: Forfeit-on-invitation-refusal branches for tournament rounds: two-plus acceptances play (forfeiters marked, score 0), exactly one is a walkover (sole acceptor 1.0), zero is a no-contest (no winner) — forfeits distinguishable from played losses by the unseated-teams marking. The non-tournament abort arm and the abstract format-override hook are game-lifecycle/launch-gates (03-REQ-056, mapped by migrate-game-lifecycle); this capability is the concrete format.

05-REVIEW-003.note: Option B — tournament-wide freeze from first-round start to final-round end; inter-round interludes remain frozen. The later decided source for the whole-event question the 03-REVIEW-006 sub-question left open; carried into the delta per this change's Open Question 1 pending author confirmation.

05-REVIEW-016.note: Orchestration reordering for forfeit semantics: the initialization-deferred-until-invitations-resolve ordering is owned by game-lifecycle/launch-orchestration#invitations-resolve-before-init and the walkover status transition by game-lifecycle/status-authority#walkover-straight-to-finished (both mapped by migrate-game-lifecycle); this capability owns the tournament-side resolution ladder and that round chaining proceeds from any branch.

05-REVIEW-018.note: Walkover score value: sole acceptor records 1.0 — par, the normalised-scoring value for a field of one — forfeiters 0; the 'winner-by-default' sentinel removed everywhere. Phrased abstractly in the delta because game-engine is outside the tournaments dependency ceiling; the formula stays single-sourced in the engine's scoring requirements.
